brief_justification: Correct answer (No) with valid causal reasoning; Multi-level analysis identifying collider bias, explaining the mechanism of spurious correlation, and tracing the causal structure; Some self-monitoring via the injected reasoning context suppressing correlation-to-causation leaps; Verification through explaining what each method would show and why; Acknowledges the hypothetical constraints but presents conclusions fairly confidently; Systematically evaluates both methods with explicit reasons for rejecting Method 1; Complete derivation from causal graph to collider identification to bias explanation, fully reproducible
